mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of rate-cut months.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to sell equities and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20d returns occur in >60% of instances where unemployment rises above the threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings revisions upward and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in retail sales and housing starts fail to produce positive 60d returns in >50% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, which triggers short-term algorithmic buying and positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months where industrial production growth exceeds its 12-month moving average.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 300 trigger expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20d returns in >65% of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds the 300 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer spending forecasts and driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative 60d returns in >50% of observed periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output and corporate revenue potential, which systematically translates to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over 5-year rolling windows or forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production dropping below the 2017 baseline index level signals structural supply contraction and weakening manufacturing demand, prompting institutional portfolio de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 60d returns occur in >60% of months where the industrial production index falls below 100.
